summary_one_line: ELF e_machine field must equal EM_AARCH64 (0x00B7 little-endian) at the 16-bit machine offset to take the switch arm

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The ELF header e_machine half-word must hold the exact 16-bit value EM_AARCH64 = 0x00B7 (low byte 0xB7, high byte 0x00) so that `switch (e_machine)` dispatches to `case EM_AARCH64:` at elf.cc:798. In the winner this lands at input bytes 0x12=0xB7, 0x13=0x00.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The cmplog winner seed carries the bytes 01 d5 b7 00 at offsets 0x10-0x13, placing 0xB7 at offset 0x12; the per-role overlay marks line 798 case EM_AARCH64 as [W] (winner-only) and the divergent-branch table shows W takes the true side (T=3 F=0) while L never does (T=0 F=29).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Every naive loser seed misses the EM_AARCH64 constant: the byte-diff shows offset 0x12 is 0xB7 only in W while L holds 00x8/03/01, so the switch falls through to `default:` (line 822, marked [L]) which returns false; the divergent-branch table shows L takes default true 26 times and EM_AARCH64 never.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's CMP-interception logs the `e_machine == EM_AARCH64` integer comparison operands into the CmpLogObserver buffer; I2SRandReplace then scans the input for the observed operand and substitutes the magic 0xB7 directly at the matching offset, solving the exact-equality roadblock in one step. naive has only havoc/token mutators with no CMP introspection, so it must blind-guess the 2-byte magic and never lands it across 10 trials.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the case EM_AARCH64 switch arm whose true side is globally blocked.
The arm is reached only by an exact-equality switch on e_machine.
The winner seed places 0xB7 at offset 0x12 within the ELF header.
Only the winner exercises the EM_AARCH64 arm; line 798 is winner-only in the per-role overlay.
Winner takes the EM_AARCH64 true side while the loser never does.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 798  T=3 F=0  T=0 F=29  case EM_AARCH64:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: At offset 0x12 the magic byte 0xB7 is present only in W; loser seeds hold other values.
Loser seeds fall through to the default arm rather than EM_AARCH64.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 822  T=0 F=3  T=26 F=3  default:

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog logs CMP operands and I2SRandReplace substitutes the matching operand into the input.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S capability — only havoc/token mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 59 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 86809c70eeb052ac` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 19 and 20 of the 21-level ancestor chain, the lower-bound signal of an I2SRandReplace find under the current build

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on the EM_AARCH64 16-bit magic where naive resolves the branch as often as cmplog, or where cmplog fails to resolve it — showing the I2S CMP substitution is not what solves the exact-equality gate.

weakest_evidence_point: The decisive seed was stored by cmplog with a BytesCopyMutator leaf op, so the I2S substitution is inferred from dash-row ancestors rather than a labeled I2SRandReplace mutation directly on the magic byte.
